+    @MainActor
+    func testStartRecordingForwardsConvertedAudioChunksToRecordingSessionCoordinator() async {
+        let settings = makeSettings()
+        let loader = AppControllerSpeechModelLoader()
+        loader.cachedModelIDs = [SpeechModelDescriptor.whisperSmallEnglish.id]
+        let hotkeyMonitor = AppControllerHotkeyMonitor()
+        let manager = SpeechModelManager(
+            catalog: [.whisperSmallEnglish],
+            loader: loader

+        let recorder = AppControllerAudioRecorder()
+        let recordingSessionCoordinator = AppControllerRecordingSessionCoordinator()
+        let controller = AppController(
+            settingsStore: settings,
+            speechModelManager: manager,
+            hotkeyMonitor: hotkeyMonitor,
+            audioRecorder: recorder,
+            makeRecordingSessionCoordinator: { recordingSessionCoordinator }
+        )
+
+        await controller.start()
+        await hotkeyMonitor.emit(.start(.pushToTalk))
+        recorder.emitChunk([0.1, -0.2, 0.3])
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(
+            recordingSessionCoordinator.processedChunks,
+            [[0.1, -0.2, 0.3]]
+        )
+    }
+

+    @MainActor
+    func testRestartRecordingClearsBufferedSamplesBelowThreshold() async {
+        let settings = makeSettings()
+        let loader = AppControllerSpeechModelLoader()
+        loader.cachedModelIDs = [SpeechModelDescriptor.whisperSmallEnglish.id]
+        let hotkeyMonitor = AppControllerHotkeyMonitor()
+        let manager = SpeechModelManager(
+            catalog: [.whisperSmallEnglish],
+            loader: loader
+        )
+        let recorder = AppControllerAudioRecorder()
+        recorder.bufferedSampleCount = 12_000
+        let overlay = AppControllerOverlayPresenter()
+        let controller = AppController(
+            settingsStore: settings,
+            speechModelManager: manager,
+            hotkeyMonitor: hotkeyMonitor,
+            audioRecorder: recorder,
+            overlayPresenter: overlay
+        )
+
+        await controller.start()
+        await hotkeyMonitor.emit(.start(.pushToTalk))
+        await hotkeyMonitor.emit(.restart(.toggleToTalk))
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(controller.status, .recording)
+        XCTAssertEqual(recorder.clearBufferedSamplesCallCount, 1)
+        XCTAssertEqual(overlay.events, [.show(.recording)])
+    }
+
+    @MainActor
+    func testRestartRecordingKeepsBufferedSamplesAtThresholdOrAbove() async {
+        let settings = makeSettings()
+        let loader = AppControllerSpeechModelLoader()
+        loader.cachedModelIDs = [SpeechModelDescriptor.whisperSmallEnglish.id]
+        let hotkeyMonitor = AppControllerHotkeyMonitor()
+        let manager = SpeechModelManager(
+            catalog: [.whisperSmallEnglish],
+            loader: loader
+        )
+        let recorder = AppControllerAudioRecorder()
+        recorder.bufferedSampleCount = 16_000
+        let overlay = AppControllerOverlayPresenter()
+        let controller = AppController(
+            settingsStore: settings,
+            speechModelManager: manager,
+            hotkeyMonitor: hotkeyMonitor,
+            audioRecorder: recorder,
+            overlayPresenter: overlay
+        )
+
+        await controller.start()
+        await hotkeyMonitor.emit(.start(.pushToTalk))
+        await hotkeyMonitor.emit(.restart(.toggleToTalk))
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(controller.status, .recording)
+        XCTAssertEqual(recorder.clearBufferedSamplesCallCount, 0)
+        XCTAssertEqual(overlay.events, [.show(.recording)])
+    }

+
+    func testKeyDownUsesModifierFlagsForExactToggleMatch() {
+        let engine = HotkeyEngine(bindings: .defaultBindings)
+        _ = engine.process(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .flagsChanged,
+                key: .rightCommand,
+                modifierFlags: [.rightCommand]
+            )
+        )
+        _ = engine.process(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .flagsChanged,
+                key: .rightOption,
+                modifierFlags: [.rightCommand, .rightOption]
+            )
+        )
+
+        let effects = engine.process(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .keyDown,
+                key: .space,
+                modifierFlags: [.rightCommand, .rightOption]
+            )
+        )
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(effects, [.restart(.toggleToTalk)])
+        XCTAssertEqual(engine.activeMode, .toggleToTalk)
+        XCTAssertEqual(engine.pressedKeys, [.rightCommand, .rightOption, .space])
+    }
+
+    func testReplacePressedKeysReevaluatesTransitions() {
+        let engine = HotkeyEngine(bindings: .defaultBindings)
+        _ = engine.process(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .flagsChanged,
+                key: .rightCommand,
+                modifierFlags: [.rightCommand]
+            )
+        )
+        _ = engine.process(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .flagsChanged,
+                key: .rightOption,
+                modifierFlags: [.rightCommand, .rightOption]
+            )
+        )
+
+        let effects = engine.replacePressedKeys([.rightCommand, .rightOption, .space])
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(effects, [.restart(.toggleToTalk)])
+        XCTAssertEqual(engine.activeMode, .toggleToTalk)
+        XCTAssertEqual(engine.pressedKeys, [.rightCommand, .rightOption, .space])
+    }
 }

+    @MainActor
+    func testNonModifierEventsAreDroppedUntilMatchingModifierFlagsAppear() async throws {
+        let factory = RecordingHotkeyEventTapSessionFactory()
+        let monitor = GlobalHotkeyMonitor(
+            accessibilityChecker: StubAccessibilityPermissionChecker(isTrusted: true),
+            eventTapSessionFactory: factory,
+            snapshotDispatcher: ImmediateHotkeySnapshotDispatcher()
+        )
+        var effects: [HotkeyEffect] = []
+
+        try monitor.start(bindings: .defaultBindings) { effect in
+            effects.append(effect)
+        }
+
+        let session = try XCTUnwrap(factory.sessions.first)
+        session.emit(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .keyDown,
+                key: .tab,
+                modifierFlags: []
+            )
+        )
+        session.emit(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .flagsChanged,
+                key: .rightCommand,
+                modifierFlags: [.rightCommand]
+            )
+        )
+        session.emit(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .flagsChanged,
+                key: .rightOption,
+                modifierFlags: [.rightCommand, .rightOption]
+            )
+        )
+        await Task.yield()
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(effects, [.start(.pushToTalk)])
+    }
+
+    @MainActor
+    func testPollingRecoveryAddsMissingChordKeysWhenCurrentEventAgrees() async throws {
+        let factory = RecordingHotkeyEventTapSessionFactory()
+        let poller = RecordingHotkeyPhysicalKeyStatePoller()
+        poller.pressedKeys = [.rightCommand, .rightOption]
+        let monitor = GlobalHotkeyMonitor(
+            accessibilityChecker: StubAccessibilityPermissionChecker(isTrusted: true),
+            eventTapSessionFactory: factory,
+            snapshotDispatcher: ImmediateHotkeySnapshotDispatcher(),
+            physicalKeyStatePoller: poller
+        )
+        var effects: [HotkeyEffect] = []
+
+        try monitor.start(bindings: .defaultBindings) { effect in
+            effects.append(effect)
+        }
+
+        let session = try XCTUnwrap(factory.sessions.first)
+        session.emit(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .flagsChanged,
+                key: .rightOption,
+                modifierFlags: [.rightOption]
+            )
+        )
         await Task.yield()
+
         XCTAssertEqual(effects, [.start(.pushToTalk)])
     }
+
+    func testDisabledTapIsImmediatelyReenabledAndLogged() throws {
+        let factory = RecordingHotkeyEventTapSessionFactory()
+        let logger = RecordingHotkeyDebugLogger()
+        let monitor = GlobalHotkeyMonitor(
+            accessibilityChecker: StubAccessibilityPermissionChecker(isTrusted: true),
+            eventTapSessionFactory: factory,
+            snapshotDispatcher: ImmediateHotkeySnapshotDispatcher(),
+            logger: logger
+        )
+
+        try monitor.start(bindings: .defaultBindings) { _ in }
+        let session = try XCTUnwrap(factory.sessions.first)
+        session.emitDisabled(.timeout)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(session.setEnabledCalls, [true])
+        XCTAssertTrue(logger.messages.contains("event tap disabled reason=timeout"))
+        XCTAssertTrue(logger.messages.contains("event tap re-enabled"))
+    }
+
+    @MainActor
+    func testHotkeyLoggerRecordsLifecycleAndEventMessages() async throws {
+        let factory = RecordingHotkeyEventTapSessionFactory()
+        let logger = RecordingHotkeyDebugLogger()
+        let poller = RecordingHotkeyPhysicalKeyStatePoller()
+        let monitor = GlobalHotkeyMonitor(
+            accessibilityChecker: StubAccessibilityPermissionChecker(isTrusted: true),
+            eventTapSessionFactory: factory,
+            snapshotDispatcher: ImmediateHotkeySnapshotDispatcher(),
+            physicalKeyStatePoller: poller,
+            logger: logger
+        )
+
+        try monitor.start(bindings: .defaultBindings) { _ in }
+        monitor.updateBindings(.defaultBindings)
+
+        let session = try XCTUnwrap(factory.sessions.first)
+        session.emit(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .flagsChanged,
+                key: .rightCommand,
+                modifierFlags: [.rightCommand]
+            )
+        )
+
+        monitor.suspend()
+        session.emit(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .flagsChanged,
+                key: .rightOption,
+                modifierFlags: [.rightCommand, .rightOption]
+            )
+        )
+        monitor.resume()
+        session.emit(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .flagsChanged,
+                key: .rightOption,
+                modifierFlags: [.rightCommand]
+            )
+        )
+        session.emit(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .flagsChanged,
+                key: .rightCommand,
+                modifierFlags: []
+            )
+        )
+        monitor.stop()
+
+        XCTAssertTrue(logger.messages.contains(where: { $0.hasPrefix("start push=") }))
+        XCTAssertTrue(logger.messages.contains(where: { $0.hasPrefix("bindings updated push=") }))
+        XCTAssertTrue(logger.messages.contains(where: { $0.contains("event=flagsChanged:⌘ʳ") }))
+        XCTAssertTrue(logger.messages.contains("suspend"))
+        XCTAssertTrue(logger.messages.contains(where: { $0.hasPrefix("key release guard armed keys=") }))
+        XCTAssertTrue(logger.messages.contains(where: { $0.hasPrefix("suppressed while suspended event=") }))
+        XCTAssertTrue(logger.messages.contains("resume"))
+        XCTAssertTrue(logger.messages.contains("key release guard cleared"))
+        XCTAssertTrue(logger.messages.contains("stop"))
+    }
+
+    @MainActor
+    func testSnapshotsAreDispatchedThroughInjectedDispatcherBeforeHandling() async throws {
+        let factory = RecordingHotkeyEventTapSessionFactory()
+        let dispatcher = RecordingHotkeySnapshotDispatcher()
+        let monitor = GlobalHotkeyMonitor(
+            accessibilityChecker: StubAccessibilityPermissionChecker(isTrusted: true),
+            eventTapSessionFactory: factory,
+            snapshotDispatcher: dispatcher
+        )
+        var effects: [HotkeyEffect] = []
+
+        try monitor.start(bindings: .defaultBindings) { effect in
+            effects.append(effect)
+        }
+
+        let session = try XCTUnwrap(factory.sessions.first)
+        session.emit(
+            HotkeyEventSnapshot(
+                kind: .flagsChanged,
+                key: .rightCommand,
+                modifierFlags: [.rightCommand]
+            )
+        )
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(dispatcher.pendingBlockCount, 1)
+        XCTAssertEqual(effects, [])
+
+        dispatcher.drain()
+        await Task.yield()
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(effects, [])
+    }
 }
 
 private struct StubAccessibilityPermissionChecker: AccessibilityPermissionChecking {
@@ -148,27 +426,87 @@ private final class RecordingHotkeyEventTapSessionFactory: HotkeyEventTapSession
     private(set) var makeCallCount = 0
     private(set) var sessions: [RecordingHotkeyEventTapSession] = []
 
-    func make(handler: @escaping @Sendable (HotkeyEventSnapshot) -> Void) throws -> any HotkeyEventTapSession {
+    func make(
+        handler: @escaping @Sendable (HotkeyEventSnapshot) -> Void,
+        disabledHandler: @escaping @Sendable (HotkeyEventTapDisableReason) -> Void
+    ) throws -> any HotkeyEventTapSession {
         makeCallCount += 1
-        let session = RecordingHotkeyEventTapSession(handler: handler)
+        let session = RecordingHotkeyEventTapSession(
+            handler: handler,
+            disabledHandler: disabledHandler
+        )
         sessions.append(session)
         return session
     }
 }
 
 private final class RecordingHotkeyEventTapSession: HotkeyEventTapSession {
     private let handler: @Sendable (HotkeyEventSnapshot) -> Void
+    private let disabledHandler: @Sendable (HotkeyEventTapDisableReason) -> Void
     private(set) var stopCallCount = 0
+    private(set) var setEnabledCalls: [Bool] = []
 
-    init(handler: @escaping @Sendable (HotkeyEventSnapshot) -> Void) {
+    init(
+        handler: @escaping @Sendable (HotkeyEventSnapshot) -> Void,
+        disabledHandler: @escaping @Sendable (HotkeyEventTapDisableReason) -> Void
+    ) {
         self.handler = handler
+        self.disabledHandler = disabledHandler
     }
 
     func emit(_ snapshot: HotkeyEventSnapshot) {
         handler(snapshot)
     }
 
+    func emitDisabled(_ reason: HotkeyEventTapDisableReason) {
+        disabledHandler(reason)
+    }
+
+    func setEnabled(_ isEnabled: Bool) {
+        setEnabledCalls.append(isEnabled)
+    }
+
     func stop() {
         stopCallCount += 1
     }
 }
+
+private final class RecordingHotkeySnapshotDispatcher: HotkeySnapshotDispatching {
+    private var pendingBlocks: [@Sendable () -> Void] = []
+
+    var pendingBlockCount: Int {
+        pendingBlocks.count
+    }
+
+    func dispatch(_ work: @escaping @Sendable () -> Void) {
+        pendingBlocks.append(work)
+    }
+
+    func drain() {
+        let blocks = pendingBlocks
+        pendingBlocks.removeAll()
+        blocks.forEach { $0() }
+    }
+}
+
+private final class ImmediateHotkeySnapshotDispatcher: HotkeySnapshotDispatching {
+    func dispatch(_ work: @escaping @Sendable () -> Void) {
+        work()
+    }
+}
+
+private final class RecordingHotkeyPhysicalKeyStatePoller: HotkeyPhysicalKeyStatePolling {
+    var pressedKeys: Set<HotkeyKey> = []
+
+    func isPressed(_ key: HotkeyKey) -> Bool {
+        pressedKeys.contains(key)
+    }
+}
+
+private final class RecordingHotkeyDebugLogger: HotkeyDebugLogging {
+    private(set) var messages: [String] = []
+
+    func log(_ message: String) {
+        messages.append(message)
+    }
+}

 - Hardened deterministic correction application and post-paste learning inference so narrow replacements are applied longest-first without overlap, learned replacements use the intended embedded phrase, duplicate learned pairs are ignored, and missing Screen Recording permission fails quietly instead of gating the whole flow.
 - Wired the real post-paste learning coordinator into `AppController`, including live paste-session polling, learned-correction overlay callbacks, OCR custom-word reuse from the correction store, and focused controller/coordinator/settings coverage for the adaptive correction path.
 **Summary:** Delivered the corrections and post-paste learning slice on top of real paste delivery, OCR context, and cleanup. Ghost Pepper now persists structured correction rules behind the Corrections settings drafts, reuses preferred transcription terms for both OCR and deterministic cleanup, learns new 1-2 word replacements from stable post-paste edits, shows a learned-correction overlay when a new rule is stored, and quietly expires the learning window when Screen Recording text is unavailable while continuing to show the documented recovery prompt in Settings. No stories were moved forward during wrap-up because the full ITER-0012 scope is now satisfied in place.
+
+## ITER-0013
+
+**Completed:** 2026-04-10
+**Stories delivered:** STORY-0049, STORY-0050, STORY-0051, STORY-0052, STORY-0053, STORY-0054, STORY-0055, STORY-0056, STORY-0057, STORY-0058, STORY-0059, STORY-0061, STORY-0063, STORY-0064, STORY-0065, STORY-0066, STORY-0067, STORY-0068, STORY-0069, STORY-0070, STORY-0071, STORY-0072, STORY-0074, STORY-0075, STORY-0076, STORY-0077, STORY-0078, STORY-0079, STORY-0080, STORY-0081, STORY-0082, STORY-0083, STORY-0084, STORY-0085, STORY-0086, STORY-0087, STORY-0088, STORY-0089, STORY-0092, STORY-0840, STORY-0841, STORY-0842
+**Tasks executed:**
+- Hardened the hotkey engine and monitor around authoritative modifier-flag snapshots, serial tap-event dispatch, non-modifier gating, additive polling recovery, post-stop reset, event-tap auto-reenable, and structured hotkey debug logging.
+- Fixed the hotkey stack regressions uncovered during the iteration by removing the monitor start-state wipe, correcting the modifier-flag array-literal recursion bug, and preserving the dedicated background tap thread and single-lock state model.
+- Added the remaining controller-side hotkey wiring by handling restart buffer thresholds in `AppController`, forwarding converted audio chunks into a per-recording session coordinator seam for future speaker filtering, and expanding controller, engine, and monitor coverage for restart, recovery, and logging paths.
+**Summary:** Delivered the hotkey hardening slice on top of the earlier shortcut and dictation loop work. Ghost Pepper now processes hotkey snapshots off the tap thread under a single monitor lock, derives modifier state from authoritative flag bitmasks, recovers missed chord keys conservatively by polling, re-enables disabled event taps immediately, logs hotkey lifecycle and event decisions, restarts recordings with the documented 16,000-sample buffer rule, and forwards converted audio chunks through a recording-session seam that keeps the later speaker-filtering runtime decoupled from the current keyboard stack. No stories were moved forward during wrap-up because the remaining speaker-filtering dependency in STORY-0054 was closed with the recording-session seam rather than deferred.
